Task 1: Treatment Effect Heterogeneity

Visual Assessment

Setup

library(tidyverse)
library(glue)
library(patchwork)
library(knitr)
library(here)
library(fs)

source(here::here("R", "task1_helpers.R"))

results_dir <- here::here("results")
data_dir <- here::here("ressources", "Datasets", "Datasets", "Blinded_data_sets")

csv_files <- get_csv_file_names(data_dir)

csv_files <- csv_files[1:5]

dataset_records <- build_task1_dataset_records(
  csv_files = csv_files,
  n_bins = params$n_bins,
  min_arm_n = params$min_arm_n
)

Scope of this document

This report is restricted to visual exploration for Task 1.

It is used to inspect whether treatment effect heterogeneity appears visually plausible within a dataset.

It does not perform:

  • final variable selection
  • patient-level subgroup assignment
  • final dataset-level automatic yes or no decisions

Numeric prioritisation is used only to sort and visually mark univariate plots for faster review.

Manifest und Review-Sheet

review_path <- fs::path(results_dir, "task1_visual_review_sheet.csv")

dataset_manifest <- build_dataset_manifest(dataset_records)

task1_review_sheet <- init_task1_review_sheet(
  dataset_manifest = dataset_manifest,
  review_path = review_path
)

knitr::kable(dataset_manifest, format = "pipe")
dataset_id endpoint n_obs n_vars binary_covariates continuous_covariates n_bins
1222 binary 150 50 20 30 4
1652 continuous 150 15 3 12 4
1964 binary 500 6 1 5 6
2119 continuous 700 6 1 5 8
2318 continuous 700 50 20 30 8

Dataset triage overview

The table below is a review aid. It helps identify datasets that contain more visually prominent univariate effect patterns. It does not replace the final Task 1 judgement.

triage_path <- fs::path(results_dir, "task1_dataset_triage.csv")

task1_dataset_triage <- build_task1_dataset_triage_table(dataset_records)

readr::write_csv2(task1_dataset_triage, triage_path, na = "")

knitr::kable(task1_dataset_triage, format = "pipe", digits = 3)
dataset_id endpoint n_obs n_vars n_bins overall_effect n_high n_medium n_low prop_high prop_medium_or_higher max_priority_score mean_priority_score top_priority_vars dataset_priority_hint
1222 binary 150 50 4 -0.053 18 22 10 0.360 0.800 1.056 0.630 x38, x20, x40, x13, x1 multiple_high_priority_signals
2318 continuous 700 50 8 2.211 5 22 23 0.100 0.540 1.005 0.435 x35, x41, x30, x28, x2 multiple_high_priority_signals
2119 continuous 700 6 8 -0.014 2 2 2 0.333 0.667 1.100 0.675 x6, x4, x3, x1, x2 high_plus_additional_support
1652 continuous 150 15 4 2.529 1 6 8 0.067 0.467 1.052 0.442 x8, x11, x3, x9, x14 high_plus_additional_support
1964 binary 500 6 6 0.260 1 2 3 0.167 0.500 0.850 0.425 x3, x4, x1, x2, x6 high_plus_additional_support

Plots by dataset

render_task1_dataset_sections(dataset_records)

All variables are shown. Numeric prioritisation is used only to order and colour the plots for faster visual review.
